settings.title: ⚙️ Questionnaire Settings
settings.sampleSizeTip: Total number of respondents in the questionnaire
settings.scaleTypeTip: Choose the number of response options

weights.title: ⚖️ Category Weights:
weights.noteText: You can modify the weights based on the scale direction (positive or negative). Default weights start from {max} in descending order.
weights.noteExample: Example: For negative scales, you can reverse the weight order.

analysis.title: 📊 Qualitative Analysis of Results
analysis.methodText: The overall analysis is based on aggregated data from all questions. Frequencies were combined and statistical indicators calculated at the instrument level as a whole, not based on averages of individual question indicators, to ensure accurate statistical representation of the overall trend of the study sample.
analysis.overviewText: Based on the statistical analysis of ({totalQ}) questions in the questionnaire, the results showed that the overall weighted arithmetic mean was ({mean}) out of ({maxWeight}), reflecting a level of {agreementLevel} from respondents, {agreementDesc}.
analysis.homogeneityText: The average standard deviation was ({std}), which indicates {homogeneityLevel} {homogeneityDesc}. The average variance was ({variance}), confirming this trend in data analysis.
analysis.consistencyText: The coefficient of variation averaged ({cv}%), representing {consistencyLevel} {consistencyDesc}. This indicator is an important relative measure for evaluating the quality of collected data.
analysis.rangeText: Weighted arithmetic mean values ranged from ({minMean}) as the lowest value to ({maxMean}) as the highest value, indicating {rangeDesc} in respondent responses across various questions. The median average was ({median}), which is close to the arithmetic mean, enhancing the credibility of the analysis.
analysis.conclusionPositive: Statistical indicators generally point to positive and consistent results reflecting clarity in respondent trends and consistency in their opinions.
analysis.conclusionNeutral: Statistical indicators generally point to acceptable results with room for improvement in some aspects.
analysis.conclusionNegative: Statistical indicators generally point to results that require reconsideration of influencing factors and addressing identified weaknesses.
analysis.highCVNote: 💡 It is recommended to conduct a deeper analysis of questions with high variation to understand the reasons for differing opinions.
analysis.agreementVeryHighDesc: indicating a strong positive trend and broad acceptance from respondents
analysis.agreementHighDesc: reflecting a notable positive trend among respondents
analysis.agreementMediumDesc: indicating a neutral trend leaning toward positive
analysis.agreementDisagreeDesc: indicating a clear negative trend among respondents
analysis.homogeneityVeryHighDesc: indicating strong consensus and significant agreement in opinions
analysis.homogeneityGoodDesc: indicating notable consistency in responses with limited variation
analysis.homogeneityMediumDesc: reflecting some variation in opinions without significant dispersion
analysis.homogeneityLowDesc: indicating clear differences in respondent viewpoints
analysis.consistencyExcellentDesc: falling within ideal standards for statistical studies
analysis.consistencyGoodDesc: falling within the acceptable range for social research
analysis.consistencyLowDesc: requiring deeper examination of influencing factors

messages.cantChangeWeights: ❌ Cannot change scale type after locking weights. Please reset.
messages.enterWeights: ⚠️ Please enter weights correctly for each category (cannot be zero).

messages.countsMismatch: ❌ Total frequencies ({total}) do not equal sample size ({sample}). Difference: {diff}
